channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Voters react to Gov. Tim Walz claiming abortion is a 'basic human right'
item.description: Fox News Digital's dial group made up of independent, Democratic and Republican voters differed on Democratic Gov. Tim Walz's comments on abortion in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump's approach to Mideast crisis, hurricane to test public mood in final weeks of campaign
item.description: A trio of new trials — a devastating hurricane, expanding conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike that threatens the U.S. economy — are looming over the final weeks of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Zeke Miller And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump is returning this weekend to the town where an assassin's bullet grazed his ear in July.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first and only vice presidential debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president's second in command.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance: Donald Trump improved Obamacare
item.description: Sen. JD Vance defended Donald Trump's health care record, claiming Trump had improved the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz repeats Georgia abortion death falsehood decried by doctors as 'fearmongering'
item.description: Tim Walz claimed during the vice presidential debate that a Georgia woman likely died due to the state's abortion laws despite doctors slamming the narrative as "fearmongering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance and Walz keep it civil in a policy-heavy discussion: VP debate takeaways
item.description: Vice presidential nominees JD Vance and Tim Walz have used their time on the debate stage to focus the bulk of their attacks not on one another, but on those at the top of their rival tickets.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Zeke Miller And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Breaking down the CD-22 race and its candidates ahead of high-stakes Wednesday debate
item.description: Kicking it off Wednesday evening is the head-to-head between Republican incumbent David Valadao and Democrat Rudy Salas for California's 22nd Congressional District.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't participate in interview for '60 Minutes' election special
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — CBS News said Tuesday that Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump has declined to participate in an interview with “60 Minutes” for its election special, which will go forward next Monday with Democratic opponent Kamala Harris alone.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson says Congress has provided funds for Helene response
item.description: “Congress has previously provided the funds it [FEMA] needs to respond, so we will make sure that those resources are appropriately allocated,” Johnson said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Education a potential flashpoint in Vance-Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Education hardly came up in last month’s Trump-Harris presidential debate, but it could be a central focus at Tuesday’s undercard event.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrat calls for Congress to return to pass disaster aid after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Tuesday called for the Senate to return from its lengthy preelection recess to pass a disaster aid package following the damage left in multiple states by Hurricane Helene. “I think we should pass it yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Get to know the candidates: We sent participants in the 22nd Congressional District Debate a questionnaire. Here’s how they responded
item.description: Each candidate participating in Wednesday's 22nd Congressional Debate responded to our questionnaire.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Gutierrez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Eric Adams appoints new chief counsel amid string of resignations
item.description: Prosecutors unsealed an indictment last week alleging Adams took bribes and illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Armando Cataldi, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania survey finds Harris leading Trump narrowly, identifies her 'biggest weakness,' pollster says
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is leading former President Donald Trump among likely voters in Pennsylvania by a razor-thin margin, according to new AARP polling data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana GOP Senate candidate Sheehy says he was in Afghanistan while Jon Tester was 'eating lobbyist steak'
item.description: Montana GOP U.S. Senate candidate Tim Sheehy criticized his opponent, incumbent Democrat Sen. Jon Tester, for his ties to lobbyists during a debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats sue over controversial Georgia voting rule
item.description: Democrats filed a lawsuit Monday looking to block a controversial rule requiring ballots to be hand-counted by local precincts in Georgia, arguing it violated Georgia law and called it “improper.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance’s time on the trail has often been overshadowed by self-made controversy
item.description: Republican Ohio Sen. JD Vance is prolific on the campaign trail with speeches and talking to reporters, but his messaging has at times been overshadowed by controversy often of his own making.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Heightened 'significance and importance' as Vance, Walz face off in VP debate
item.description: With a 2nd face-to-face showdown between Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump unlikely, there's a lot on the line in the vice presidential debate with five weeks until Election Day
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why fracking in Pennsylvania is a major campaign issue
item.description: Fracking — extracting oil and natural gas from underground formations — is one of the major campaign issues.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Harris and Trump change their campaign plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is scrambling the presidential candidates' plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm's impact.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, JILL COLVIN and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats fight to hang on to Nevada in Harris-Trump race
item.description: Democrats are fighting tooth and nail to keep Nevada from slipping away from them this cycle after narrowly winning it in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
